analysis:
1. NVIDIA Price Targets: Confirmed Dips, Upside Rejected

NVIDIA has already breached the $192 and $184 downside targets at 99.9% each, and the $176 dip market also sits at 99.9%, confirming sustained downward price action through March. The $164 dip is now priced at 30% and $152 at 15%, indicating markets see limited probability of a deeper collapse below current levels. Upside markets are emphatically rejected: $260 is at 0.8%, $280 at 0.5%, and $208 at 14% — all with sharp intraday declines today (the $260 target dropped 47pp, $280 dropped 45.5pp, $260 dropped 46.95pp today alone). The $200 recovery target sits at just 20%, down 3pp today, underscoring that a meaningful near-term rebound is not expected.

2. Google and Tesla Downside Pressure Dominant

Google's $300 dip market is at 99.9% (up 8pp today), and the $290 dip sits at 31% (up 5pp today), signaling continued bearish pressure on GOOGL in March. Upside targets are collapsing: $320 dropped 8.5pp and $330 dropped 8.5pp today, both now at 41% and 35% respectively, while $375 is at just 4.3% after a 43.5pp intraday collapse. Tesla's $390 and $405 dip markets are effectively resolved at 99.9% and 99.6%, and the $375 dip sits at 68% — but deep downside ($263 dip) is only 1.1%, down 8pp today. Tesla closing above $390 end of March is priced at 58%, consistent with a market hovering around that level without clear directional conviction.

3. S&P 500 Macro Range: Bears Control the Narrative

The SPX $6,600 low target for March is at 82% and $6,500 is at 62%, while $6,300 sits at 38% and $6,000 at 17% — a probability curve consistent with markets pricing a meaningful drawdown but not a crash. The high-end targets are nearly dead: $7,000 is at 23%, $7,300 at 12.8%, $7,500 at 1.1%, and $8,000 at 0.6%. The extreme low of $5,000 is at just 1.6%, meaning total collapse scenarios remain off the table. The distribution strongly implies consensus around the 6,300–6,600 range for the month's trough.

4. Earnings Season: High Beat Expectations Across the Board

Micron (MU) is priced at 97.5% to beat earnings ahead of its March 18 report, the highest-conviction earnings call in the dataset. Playboy (PLBY) jumped 15.5pp today to 98.2% to beat, resolving imminently on March 16. Dollar Tree (DLTR) sits at 94% to beat (March 16), FedEx (FDX) at 95% (March 19), Accenture (ACN) at 90.5% (March 19), Lululemon (LULU) at 90% (March 17), and General Mills (GIS) at 70% — all reflecting broadly bullish earnings sentiment across consumer, tech, and logistics sectors. Meta closing above $640 end of March is priced at 65%, consistent with the stock holding its recent range.

5. Key Shifts This Week

Beyond Meat (BYND) is the sole market with a confirmed week_change, and it is dramatic: up 36.5pp this week to 17%, with an additional 9pp gain today on $202K volume — the highest-volume market in the dataset. This sharp reversal suggests a surprise earnings miss was confirmed or strongly anticipated after the market initially priced higher odds of a beat. The NVIDIA intraday moves are the most aggressive single-day shifts: $116 dip dropped 44pp, $260 target dropped 46.95pp, $280 dropped 45.5pp, and $260 dropped 47pp today, all resolving as these price levels became unreachable given current NVIDIA trading levels. Google $375 collapsed 43.5pp intraday to just 4.3%, confirming that a significant upside move in GOOGL this month is now effectively ruled out by the market.
